description_long: This is a hand axe featuring a robust, weathered wooden handle with a natural, slightly curved shape, showing signs of age and use. The handle is thick and ergonomically contoured, likely for a comfortable grip, and displays a rough, grainy wood texture. The axe head is made of dark, rusted metal, with a broad, wedge-shaped blade and a flat poll on the opposite side. The blade edge is straight and wide, suitable for chopping, and the metal surface is visibly worn and oxidized. The head is mounted onto the handle in a traditional style, with the wood passing through the eye of the blade and likely wedged for security. No visible markings, engravings, or labels are present on either the handle or the blade.
description: A weathered hand axe with a curved wooden handle and a rusted, wedge-shaped metal blade, showing signs of heavy use and age.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the curved wooden handle and the broad, rusted metal blade.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the rectangular cross-section of the handle and the thickness of the blade.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the handle's curvature and the flat poll of the axe head.
description_view_3: Bottom-up view showing the narrow edge of the blade and the end of the handle.
attribute_handle_type: Curved, thick wooden handle with a natural grain, oval cross-section, and no added grip features.
attribute_blade_design: Single broad wedge-shaped blade with a straight edge and flat poll, no beard or decorative features.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with the handle passing through the eye of the blade.
